saṃ-√śudh P. -śudhyati, to become completely pure or purified,  MW.  :
Caus. -śodhayati, to purify or cleanse thoroughly, clear,  MBh. ;  Rājat. ;
to clear (expenses), pay off,  R. ;  Kathās. ;
to clear, secure (as a road against attack),  Mn. vii, 155 ;
to subtract,  VarBṛS. ;
to divide,  Gaṇit.

saṃśudh 4 P. To be completely purified.
— Caus.
1 To purify completely.
2 To clear, pay off (expenses).
3 To correct, rectify.
4 To examine, inquire into, investigate.
